summary: Independent QA review accepts M2-CONTENT-GUARDS-001. The actual diff adds compile/schema guards for malformed and zero-area M2 polygons, invalid district and settlement anchors, duplicate stable IDs, disconnected M2 district route graphs, invalid route endpoint references, cross-content references, displayNameKey/sourceId mismatches, and nested provenance schema paths. Positive tests, bad-map negative tests, bad-reference negative tests, scope/path checks, dependency safety, and M2 world-fixture stability are satisfied.

tests_run.result: Default content validation passed: m1.abstract-graph-30 nodes=30 edges=60 manifestHash=4a438525.
M2 fixture validation passed: districts=30 settlements=10 regionalSeasonalCurves=4 routes=42 mapGeometries=40 manifestHash=9ab72b0b.

tests_run.command: node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module -e "import { readFile } from 'node:fs/promises'; import { compileContentPackV0OrThrow } from './apps/content-tools/src/index.ts'; const source=JSON.parse(await readFile('content-source/m2-fixtures/prototype-world-30-districts.json','utf8')); const first=compileContentPackV0OrThrow(source); const second=compileContentPackV0OrThrow(structuredClone(source)); if (first.kind!=='runtime-m2-world-content-pack-v0'||second.kind!=='runtime-m2-world-content-pack-v0') throw new Error('expected M2 runtime packs'); const result={manifestHash:first.manifest.manifestHash,sameHash:first.manifest.manifestHash===second.manifest.manifestHash,sameDistrictOrder:JSON.stringify(first.districts.map(d=>d.sourceId))===JSON.stringify(second.districts.map(d=>d.sourceId)),sameSettlementOrder:JSON.stringify(first.settlements.map(s=>s.sourceId))===JSON.stringify(second.settlements.map(s=>s.sourceId)),sameRouteOrder:JSON.stringify(first.routes.map(r=>r.sourceId))===JSON.stringify(second.routes.map(r=>r.sourceId))}; console.log(JSON.stringify(result)); if(result.manifestHash!=='9ab72b0b'||!result.sameHash||!result.sameDistrictOrder||!result.sameSettlementOrder||!result.sameRouteOrder) process.exitCode=1;"

node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module -e "import { readFile } from 'node:fs/promises'; import { compileContentPackV0 } from './apps/content-tools/src/index.ts'; const base=JSON.parse(await readFile('content-source/m2-fixtures/prototype-world-30-districts.json','utf8')); const run=(name,mutate,expected)=>{ const source=structuredClone(base); mutate(source); const result=compileContentPackV0(source); const got=new Set(result.errors.map((error)=>error.code+':'+error.path)); const missing=expected.filter(([code,path])=>!got.has(code+':'+path)); if(missing.length>0){ console.error(name+' missing '+JSON.stringify(missing)+' got '+JSON.stringify(result.errors)); process.exitCode=1; } else { console.log(name+' ok '+expected.map(([code,path])=>code+':'+path).join(' | ')); } }; run('malformed-polygon', s=>{ s.mapGeometries[1].points=s.mapGeometries[1].points.slice(0,2); }, [['invalid-geometry','mapGeometries[1].points']]); run('zero-area-polygon', s=>{ s.mapGeometries[0].points=[{x:0,y:0},{x:10,y:10},{x:20,y:20}]; }, [['invalid-geometry','mapGeometries[0].points']]); run('district-anchor-outside', s=>{ s.mapGeometries[0].anchor={x:999999,y:999999}; }, [['invalid-geometry','mapGeometries[0].anchor']]); run('settlement-anchor-outside', s=>{ s.mapGeometries[31].anchor={x:999999,y:999999}; }, [['invalid-geometry','mapGeometries[31].anchor']]); run('duplicate-stable-ids', s=>{ s.routes[1].sourceId='route-001'; }, [['duplicate-id','routes[1].sourceId']]); run('disconnected-route-graph', s=>{ s.routes=s.routes.filter((route)=>route.fromDistrictId!=='district-030'&&route.toDistrictId!=='district-030'); }, [['isolated-district','districts[29].sourceId']]); run('invalid-route-endpoints', s=>{ s.routes[0].fromDistrictId='district-999'; s.routes[1].toDistrictId='district-999'; }, [['bad-reference','routes[0].fromDistrictId'],['bad-reference','routes[1].toDistrictId']]); run('cross-content-refs', s=>{ s.districts[0].regionalCurveId='curve-999'; s.settlements[0].districtId='district-999'; s.mapGeometries[0].ownerId='district-999'; }, [['bad-reference','districts[0].regionalCurveId'],['bad-reference','settlements[0].districtId'],['bad-reference','mapGeometries[0].ownerId']]); run('localization-keys', s=>{ s.districts[0].displayNameKey='content.m2.prototype.district_002'; s.settlements[0].displayNameKey='content.m2.prototype.settlement_002'; s.regionalSeasonalCurves[0].displayNameKey='content.m2.prototype.curve_002'; }, [['bad-reference','districts[0].displayNameKey'],['bad-reference','settlements[0].displayNameKey'],['bad-reference','regionalSeasonalCurves[0].displayNameKey']]); run('manifest-provenance', s=>{ s.provenance={sourceCategory:'production-content',confidence:'HIGH',policyId:'MISSING-POLICY'}; }, [['invalid-schema','provenance.sourceCategory'],['invalid-schema','provenance.confidence'],['invalid-schema','provenance.policyId']]);"
tests_run.result: All independent negative mutations returned expected code:path pairs.

tests_run.command: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const files=['project/tasks/active/M2-CONTENT-GUARDS-001.json','content-source/m2-fixtures/validation-only-bad-fixtures.manifest.json']; for (const f of files) JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8')); const manifest=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(files[1],'utf8')); const failures=[]; if (manifest.kind!=='m2.validation-only-bad-fixture-manifest') failures.push('kind'); if (manifest.historicity!=='FICTIONAL') failures.push('historicity'); if (manifest.provenance?.sourceCategory!=='validation-only-fixture'||manifest.provenance?.confidence!=='LOW'||manifest.provenance?.policyId!=='M2-MAP-SOURCE-POLICY-001') failures.push('provenance'); if (!Array.isArray(manifest.scopeNotes)||!manifest.scopeNotes.join(' ').includes('Not production content')) failures.push('scopeNotes'); if (!Array.isArray(manifest.cases)||manifest.cases.length!==5) failures.push('cases'); for (const c of manifest.cases||[]) if (!c.caseId||!['bad-map','bad-reference'].includes(c.category)||c.sourceFixture!=='prototype-world-30-districts.json'||!Array.isArray(c.expectedErrorCodes)||!Array.isArray(c.expectedPaths)||c.expectedErrorCodes.length===0||c.expectedPaths.length===0) failures.push('case:'+JSON.stringify(c)); if (failures.length) { console.error(failures.join(',')); process.exit(1); } console.log('JSON parse/shape ok files='+files.length+' cases='+manifest.cases.length);"

decisions: Accepted the implementation because compiler/schema behavior and tests satisfy all M2-CONTENT-GUARDS-001 acceptance criteria.
Treated validation-only bad fixture material as acceptable because it is a manifest plus test-driven mutations, explicitly FICTIONAL/LOW and tied to M2-MAP-SOURCE-POLICY-001.
Did not require a writer fix for lead/coordination state diffs because forbidden product/runtime paths, dependency files, sim-core, UI, historical content, and docs gates are unchanged.
risks: M2 connectivity currently rejects any disconnected district because there is no M2 explicit-isolation marker. This matches the task acceptance criteria; future intentional island support should add an explicit schema marker and tests.

The validation-only bad fixture material is a manifest and test-driven mutations of the valid fixture, not standalone bad production-like fixtures. The manifest clearly marks the cases as validation-only FICTIONAL data.

notes: Implementation/test changes are in content-schema, content-tools, tests, task metadata, messages, and the validation-only M2 fixture manifest. Forbidden tracked product/runtime paths have no diff.

Workspace also contains lead/coordination state diffs in project/goal-mode-state.json, project/model-routing-state.json, project/tasks/thread-registry.json, and untracked route messages. These are not implementation artifacts and do not change product/runtime behavior.
No package.json or pnpm-lock.yaml diff was present. No sim-core, UI, Electron, historical content, ADR, gate, roadmap, manifest checksum, or validation report path changed.
Valid M2 fixture still validates with manifestHash 9ab72b0b and repeated compile preserves district, settlement, and route order.
